---
title: Schema overview
description: The top-level structure of a Basalt template.
---

A template is one JSON object, validated against
[`template.schema.json`](https://basalt.host/schemas/template.schema.json). Reference the schema
in your file to get editor completion:

```json
{ "$schema": "https://basalt.host/schemas/template.schema.json" }
```

## Top-level properties

<TypeTable
  type={{
    id: {
      description:
        "Unique identifier for the template. Generate one with `uuidgen`.",
      type: "string (uuid)",
      required: true,
    },
    game: {
      description:
        'The game this template runs, e.g. "Minecraft". Used for grouping in the panel.',
      type: "string",
      required: true,
    },
    name: {
      description: "Display name of the template.",
      type: "string",
      required: true,
    },
    description: {
      description: "Short description shown in the template picker.",
      type: "string",
      required: true,
    },
    version: {
      description:
        "Template version, semver-shaped (`major.minor.patch`). Bump it when you change the template.",
      type: "string",
      required: true,
    },
    author: {
      description: "Who maintains this template (min 3 characters).",
      type: "string",
      required: true,
    },
    logo: {
      description: "URL of a logo image shown in the picker.",
      type: "string (uri)",
      required: true,
    },
    settings: {
      description:
        "Versions, settings form, file browser rules and playit config.",
      type: "Settings",
      required: true,
    },
    docker: {
      description: "Image, environment variables and optional startup command.",
      type: "Docker",
      required: true,
    },
    backups: {
      description: "Backup targets users can archive.",
      type: "Backups",
    },
  }}
/>

No additional properties are allowed anywhere in the document; the schema is strict, which keeps
typos from silently doing nothing.

## The `settings` object

<TypeTable
  type={{
    versions: {
      description:
        "Game versions this template can deploy. Shown as a version picker; at least one required.",
      type: "string[]",
      required: true,
    },
    categories: {
      description:
        "The settings form: a tree of categories containing fields. At least one required.",
      type: "Category[]",
      required: true,
    },
    fileBrowser: {
      description:
        "Scopes the file browser: rootFolder, readOnlyPaths, hiddenPaths.",
      type: "object",
    },
    playit: {
      description:
        "Playit.gg tunnel support: enabled, protocol (tcp/udp/both), port.",
      type: "object",
    },
  }}
/>

### File browser rules

```json
"fileBrowser": {
  "rootFolder": "/data",
  "readOnlyPaths": ["/data/eula.txt"],
  "hiddenPaths": ["/data/libraries", "/data/.cache"]
}
```

- `rootFolder`: where browsing starts; users never see anything above it.
- `readOnlyPaths`: visible but protected from edits and deletion.
- `hiddenPaths`: not shown at all.

## Versioning your template

`version` identifies the template revision, while `settings.versions` lists the **game** versions
it can run; don't confuse the two. Instances record the template version they were deployed with,
and only pick up newer revisions when re-deployed.
